description:
## Goal

Introduce a refcounted off-heap zone for large binaries, with per-process MSO ("Mixed Set / Off-heap") lists tracking which heap objects reference shared bins. Lift the 64-byte hard cap on per-process heap objects in the same arc, so that ordinary large objects (tuples, maps, vectors) can live on the process heap and only true shared data (binaries) lives off-heap.

## Why

Today, every cross-process message is deep-copied. Large binaries pay full byte-copy cost on every send. The 64-byte per-object cap also forbids ordinary large objects (3+ entry maps, big bitstrings) outright. Both limits exist because the runtime has nowhere else to put large or shared data.

The BEAM solves this with refcounted off-heap binaries threaded onto per-process MSO lists. Each large binary lives once; processes that reference it carry small stubs ("ProcBin" in BEAM nomenclature) that retain/release the refcount across GC.

fz inherits the architectural property that makes this clean: at GC time, off-heap pointers are detected by the existing range check in `ptr_in_from_space` and pass through Cheney untouched. The MSO sweep is a small post-Cheney pass that walks the per-heap list once and acts on forwarding markers — no new tracing logic.

## What it looks like after this epic

- `SharedBin`: a Box-allocated refcounted struct holding `{ refcount: AtomicUsize, bit_len: u64, bytes: Box<[u8]> }`. Lives outside any process heap; freed when refcount hits zero. No global registry, no mutex — just atomic refcounts on the bins themselves.
- `HeapKind::ProcBin`: a 32-byte heap object (HeapHeader + shared_ptr + pad) that lives on a process heap and references a SharedBin. Has no FzValue children; Cheney trace is a no-op.
- `Heap::mso_list: Vec<*mut HeapHeader>`: every ProcBin allocated on a heap is pushed here. After Cheney's BFS scan and before from-space is freed, the GC walks this list — surviving ProcBins (FORWARDED_KIND in their from-space header) update their list entry to the to-space copy; dead ProcBins have their shared_ptr released and the entry dropped.
- `deep_copy_value` recognises ProcBin: instead of copying bytes, it retains the shared_ptr and allocates a fresh ProcBin in the destination heap (which pushes onto the receiver's MSO).
- `Heap::alloc_bitstring` routes bitstrings above a 64-byte threshold through `shared_bin_alloc` + `alloc_procbin` instead of inline storage. Render and bit-match dispatch on HeapKind via small helpers (`bitstring_bit_len`, `bitstring_byte_ptr`).
- `MAX_HEAP_OBJECT_BYTES` cap is gone. Ordinary large objects (tuples with many fields, maps with many entries, vecs) live on the per-process heap, bounded only by SIZE_TABLE's largest entry (~6 MB).

## Why this is clean

- **No write barriers.** fz values are immutable; no in-place pointer mutation. The MSO list is touched only at alloc (push), GC (sweep), and send (push to receiver). All known sync points.
- **No global lock.** Each SharedBin is its own allocation; refcount is atomic; the runtime allocator handles concurrent `Box::new` from any worker.
- **No new GC tracing.** Off-heap pointers fail `ptr_in_from_space` and are skipped by existing Cheney. The MSO sweep is a flat per-heap pass that reads forwarding markers — same primitive Cheney already maintains.
- **Three-path uniform.** Interpreter, JIT, and AOT all reach the runtime through `Heap::alloc_bitstring` and the helper dispatchers. One implementation, three callers.

## Subticket DAG

```
  fz-cty.1 (lift-cap) ─────────────────────────┐
                                               ▼
  fz-cty.2 (SharedBin) → fz-cty.3 (ProcBin+MSO+sweep)
                              ▼
                         fz-cty.4 (deep_copy ProcBin)
                              ▼
                         fz-cty.5 (alloc_bitstring policy + dispatchers)
                              ▼
                         fz-cty.6 (three-path acceptance) ◄────┘
```

- fz-cty.1 and fz-cty.2 are independent and may proceed in parallel.
- fz-cty.3 combines ProcBin allocation, MSO bookkeeping, and the GC sweep — each is incoherent without the others.
- fz-cty.6 depends on both .1 (large in-heap objects) and .5 (shared-zone bitstrings) for the full acceptance scenario.

## Invariants

- Every live ProcBin in a heap is in that heap's mso_list exactly once.
- Every SharedBin's refcount equals the number of live ProcBins across all heaps referencing it.
- Cross-process send increments the SharedBin refcount; per-process death decrements (via Heap::drop or GC sweep).
- Bitstring semantics (equality, render, bit-match) are identical whether storage is inline Bitstring or ProcBin.
title: shared-heap: refcounted off-heap binaries via SharedBin + ProcBin + MSO
